steps.title: Use the discriminant
steps.content: For $x^2-12x+27=0$, the discriminant is $$(-12)^2-4(1)(27)=144-108=36$$ Since $36>0$, the quadratic has two distinct real solutions.

steps.title: Confirm with a graph
steps.content: Graph $y=x^2-12x+27$ in Desmos. The graph crosses the $x$-axis twice, matching the positive discriminant. Therefore the equation has exactly two distinct real solutions.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A says there are two distinct real solutions. The other choices do not match the positive discriminant, so A is correct.
